purpose: Environment-specific guidance for locating notebook exercise/task units and determining NOT_STARTED vs HAS_ATTEMPTED.

primary_boundaries: markdown headers such as Part, Exercise, Step, Question, [Graded], Task, Problem
code cells with matching instructor-reference solution regions
function definitions or notebook cells that align to a single graded prompt
task_detection.solution_alignment_rule: If the instructor reference contains ### BEGIN SOLUTION and ### END SOLUTION, treat the corresponding region in the matching student cell as the canonical student work zone, even when the student notebook lacks explicit # YOUR CODE HERE markers.

non_function_contexts.guidance: For top-level code exercises, use the markdown instruction cell plus the following code cell as one task when they clearly belong together.
For notebooks where each step is one markdown cell followed by one code cell, treat each pair as a separate task.
For SQL tasks, treat one query cell as one task. Use SQL comment markers like -- YOUR CODE HERE when present.
If instructions explicitly say a task spans multiple cells, group those cells into one task.

multiple_functions_under_one_header.rule: When one section header introduces multiple functions in separate code cells, treat each function as a separate task.
multiple_functions_under_one_header.title_pattern: Use the section header plus the function name in the task title.
multiple_functions_under_one_header.notes_guidance: If they share a section header or shared test cell, mention that in notes.

task_detection.ambiguity_rule: When boundaries are ambiguous, prefer markdown headers first, then instructor-reference solution structure, then separate code cells as independent tasks.

explicit_markers: # YOUR CODE HERE ... # END OF YOUR CODE
-- YOUR CODE HERE ... -- END OF YOUR CODE
work_zone_rules.fallback_rule: If the student notebook lacks explicit markers, use the instructor reference solution span when available. Only if neither source gives markers, treat the cell body minus signatures, docstrings, imports, setup scaffolding, and return statements as the work zone.

has_attempted: operations or expressions that do real work
control flow such as loops or conditionals
not_started: debugging statements only, such as print("test") with no real task logic
special_cases: Code outside the ideal work zone still counts as HAS_ATTEMPTED if it is clearly the student's attempt. Note that in notes.
Commented-out placeholders plus real code still count as HAS_ATTEMPTED.

examples.label: python_not_started
examples.code:
def calculate_average(numbers):
    """Calculate the average of a list."""
    # YOUR CODE HERE
    raise NotImplementedError()
    # END OF YOUR CODE
    return avg
examples.why: The student work zone contains only a placeholder.

examples.label: python_attempted_with_bug
examples.code:
def calculate_average(numbers):
    """Calculate the average of a list."""
    # YOUR CODE HERE
    total = sum(numbers)
    avg = total
    #raise NotImplementedError()
    # END OF YOUR CODE
    return avg
examples.why: The student work zone contains implementation code even though it is wrong.

examples.label: r_not_started
examples.why: The student work zone contains only a placeholder.

examples.label: r_attempted_with_bug
examples.why: The student work zone contains implementation code.

examples.label: sql_not_started
examples.code:
-- YOUR CODE HERE
-- Write a query to find average salary by department
-- END OF YOUR CODE
examples.why: The task region contains only comments and placeholders.

examples.label: sql_attempted_with_bug
examples.code:
-- YOUR CODE HERE
SELECT department, salary
FROM employees
-- END OF YOUR CODE
examples.why: The query is real implementation even though it is incomplete or incorrect.

examples.label: script_based_notebook_task
examples.code:
# YOUR CODE HERE
data = pd.read_csv("data.csv")
mean_value = data["column"].mean()
# END OF YOUR CODE
examples.why: Top-level code in a non-function context still counts as implementation.

examples.label: single_line_attempt
examples.why: A single meaningful implementation line is enough.

examples.label: comment_only_not_started
examples.code:
# D = sqrt(S + R - 2G)
# maybe compute shape here
examples.why: Comment-only edits do not count as implementation.

shared_header_example.header: Part Two: Implement calculate_S and calculate_R
shared_header_example.guidance: Treat calculate_S and calculate_R as separate tasks. If one is attempted and the other is not, record separate statuses and note any shared test cell.

output_mapping_hints.location_guidance: Use student and solution cell indices for notebook tasks.
